metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cited reference (link to Claude Code CLI changelog/docs, version range, or a shell probe in the install guide) confirming that `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env-injection variable and that appending `export …` lines with `>>` is its supported semantics.
findings.finding: The plan's entire injection mechanism rests on `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` being the correct Claude Code CLI env-injection variable name and append-with-`>>` being its documented semantics, but `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` appears zero times anywhere in the codebase (src/, docs/, tests/, scripts/). This is an empirical claim about an external API (Claude Code CLI's own hook contract), and it cannot be verified from the repo. If the actual env var name differs (e.g. it's `CLAUDE_HOOKS_ENV_FILE`, or it uses a socket/JSON-pipe instead of a file), the shim silently no-ops for all users.
findings.location: What section — 'when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set, APPENDS … to it (the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism — append with `>>` so other hooks' vars survive)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a citation (link to Claude Code hook documentation + version) verifying that `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env-injection variable name and that append-with-`>>` is its documented semantics, or document how this was confirmed.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is 'the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism' (with append-via-`>>` semantics), but this env var name appears zero times anywhere in the repo and no citation, version reference, or external link is provided. If the Claude Code CLI does not expose an env var by this exact name — or exposes it under a different name — the entire shim silently no-ops for every user with no diagnostic signal.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set, APPENDS … to it (the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism — append with `>>` so other hooks' vars survive)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a comment in the plan (and script header) citing the specific Claude Code docs page/version that documents `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`, e.g.: '`CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`: documented at https://docs.anthropic.com/claude-code/hooks as of CLI vX.Y.Z — the file path Claude Code reads to inject env vars into the session after each hook fires.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and cite (with Claude Code version) that the `SessionStart` hook stdin JSON uses field name `session_id` and that `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is exported into hook subprocesses, adding these citations to the plan.
findings.finding: The plan makes two additional unverified capability assertions about the Claude Code hook runtime API: (1) that the `SessionStart` hook delivers a JSON envelope with a field named `session_id` (lowercase_snake) on stdin; and (2) that `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is exported into the hook subprocess's environment. Neither env var/field name appears anywhere in the repo, and no citation or version pin is given. Both names are implementation details of an external tool that may differ.
findings.location: What section — 'Reads the SessionStart JSON envelope from stdin (field `session_id`)' and 'suffixed with `_<version>` (e.g. claude-code_1.2.3) when `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is available'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing to the full implementation, verify `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct Claude Code env-injection var name and that append-with-`>>` is the documented semantics (cite the Claude Code docs version or the specific source, e.g. the official `claude.ai/docs` hooks page or the CLI `--help` output); if unconfirmed, add a SPIKE task and gate the implementation on it.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` — the single env var the entire shim depends on for env injection — appears nowhere in the codebase and the plan provides no citation (no link, no version tag, no Claude Code docs reference). If the actual Claude Code CLI uses a different var name or a different injection mechanism, the shim silently no-ops for every local user (the exact failure mode the feature is meant to prevent).
findings.location: What section: 'when `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is set, APPENDS … to it (the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism — append with `>>` so other hooks' vars survive)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Verification' sub-section to the plan citing the exact Claude Code documentation URL and version that describes `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` and the append mechanism. If the verification cannot be done in-plan, add a SPIKE story scoped to 'confirm hook env-injection API surface' before the implementation story is committed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite or embed the Claude Code hook stdin JSON schema (the exact field name for the session identifier in the `SessionStart` envelope) from official documentation or an empirical test, and add it to the plan before implementation.
findings.finding: The `SessionStart` hook stdin JSON envelope format (specifically the field name `session_id`) is asserted but unverified. No Claude Code documentation reference, version, or prior usage exists in the codebase. If the actual field is named differently (e.g. `sessionId`, `id`, or nested under a key), the python3 parser will silently produce a blank/missing session_id and the shim will no-op.
findings.location: What section: 'Reads the SessionStart JSON envelope from stdin (field `session_id`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC6's proof assertions (or add a sub-bullet) to also verify that `docs/event-schema.md` and `session_id.py` no longer contain the old `claude-code_<ver>` literal form.
findings.finding: AC6's proof command (`pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q`) asserts only that `docs/config.md` drops the old form and gains `base name 'claude-code'`. The What section names two additional deliverables — `docs/event-schema.md` and `session_id.py` — that also receive vocabulary-alignment edits, but AC6 provides no verification for them, leaving those described changes uncovered by any criterion.
findings.location: AC6 proof vs What section ('Update the `claude-code_<ver>` examples in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and the `session_id.py` comment')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC6, after the docs/config.md assertion, add: 'AND `docs/event-schema.md` and `session_id.py` no longer contain the literal string `claude-code_<ver>` and each contain the phrase `base name` or `optional _<version> suffix`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` to the plan's Scope/What edit list and specify that `test_docs_document_provenance` must be extended with assertions that (a) `docs/config.md` no longer contains the old `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` slash-form and (b) does contain the phrase `base name \`claude-code\``.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` is an unenumerated edit target: AC6 cites `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q` as proof of vocabulary alignment, but the existing `test_docs_document_provenance` function only asserts presence of `AI_AGENT` and `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` — it does NOT assert the slash-form removal or the new `base name \`claude-code\`` phrase. Satisfying the AC as written requires adding assertions to that test, yet the file appears nowhere in the plan's What/Scope sections.
findings.location: AC6 (vocabulary alignment proof) / Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a bullet: 'Update `test_docs_document_provenance` in `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` to assert (a) `"claude-code_<ver>" / "opencode"` slash-form is absent from `docs/config.md` and (b) the string `base name \`claude-code\`` is present.' This makes AC6's `-k docs` proof command actually exercise the required invariants.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` to the Scope's in-scope file list (alongside `test_session_shims.py`) so the file AC6 relies on is explicitly planned.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists 'a unit test validating the script' and names only `tests/unit/test_session_shims.py` implicitly, but AC6's proof command is `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q`. An implementer reading the Scope would not plan to touch `test_harness_provenance.py`, yet AC6 requires changes to it — a contradiction between the Scope's enumeration of in-scope files and the AC verification step.
findings.location: Scope section ('a unit test validating the script') vs AC6 proof ('pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, extend 'a unit test validating the script' to read: 'a unit test validating the script (`tests/unit/test_session_shims.py`) and additions to `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` asserting the vocabulary-alignment doc edits.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the idempotency strip is scoped to 'lines written by this hook only' (and if so, specify the mechanism — e.g., sentinel comments, unique tag) or acknowledge that it strips ALL lines with those variable names (and state that REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT are owned exclusively by rebar shims so no other hook should write them).
findings.finding: The idempotency strip-rewrite mechanism greps/filters the env file by variable name (`REBAR_SESSION_ID`, `AI_AGENT`), but the plan claims it 'STRIPS this hook's own prior exports' — the implementation cannot distinguish this hook's writes from another hook's writes of the same variable name. In a multi-hook setup where a different hook also exports `REBAR_SESSION_ID`, re-firing the claude-code hook will silently overwrite the other hook's value, contrary to the 'preserving other hooks' lines' guarantee that the plan makes.
findings.location: What section — Idempotency description: 're-firing on resume/clear/compact first STRIPS this hook's own prior `REBAR_SESSION_ID`/`AI_AGENT` exports from the env file (preserving other hooks' lines)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) mark this hook's written lines with a trailing sentinel comment (e.g., `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=abc123  # rebar-claude-code-shim`) and strip only lines with that sentinel, preserving any other hook's export of the same var; or (b) state explicitly in the plan that REBAR_SESSION_ID and AI_AGENT are rebar-owned vars that no other hook should write, making the strip-by-name safe by convention.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a canonical, exhaustive `AI_AGENT` / `claim_harness` vocabulary table (all known values, version-suffix optionality rule) to `docs/config.md` or `session_id.py`, and reference it from the vocabulary-alignment bullet so future shim authors and consumers have a single source of truth.
findings.finding: The plan introduces bare `claude-code` (unversioned) as a new valid `AI_AGENT` / `claim_harness` value, but the complete updated canonical vocabulary for that field is never defined anywhere: the existing enum examples in `session_id.py`, `docs/config.md`, and `docs/event-schema.md` all use the versioned form `claude-code_<ver>`, and `ticket_state.schema.json` declares `claim_harness` as an opaque `string|null` with no enum constraint. Downstream consumers that pattern-match on the harness tag (e.g. to distinguish harnesses) have no authoritative reference for the full updated vocabulary.
findings.location: What section ('The harness tag follows the established AI_AGENT vocabulary: the base name `claude-code`, suffixed with `_<version>`…') + Vocabulary alignment bullet + src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py line 45 + ticket_state.schema.json line 24
findings.suggested_fix: In `session_id.py`, replace the inline example comment with a reference to a `_KNOWN_HARNESS_PREFIXES` tuple (e.g. `('claude-code', 'opencode', 'codex', 'cursor')`) and document the rule: 'bare name is always valid; `_<semver>` suffix is optional'. Reference this tuple from the vocabulary-alignment doc edits so the full vocabulary is formally defined in one place.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact line-matching rule for the idempotency strip step (e.g. 'remove any line matching `^export REBAR_SESSION_ID=` or `^export AI_AGENT=`') and document whether this intentionally removes manually-set values, so the executor implements the correct behavior and the AC4 test covers the correct edge cases.
findings.finding: The idempotency strip-and-rewrite protocol says the script 'first STRIPS this hook's own prior `REBAR_SESSION_ID`/`AI_AGENT` exports from the env file (preserving other hooks' lines)', but the plan never specifies the pattern/regex used to identify which lines belong to 'this hook' vs. any other source — a processing-rule gap that makes the 'preserving other hooks' lines' guarantee unenforced.
findings.location: What section, Hook script bullet: 'Idempotent: re-firing on resume/clear/compact first STRIPS this hook's own prior `REBAR_SESSION_ID`/`AI_AGENT` exports from the env file (preserving other hooks' lines) then re-writes the current values'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Hook script bullet: 'Strip pattern: any line whose content matches `^export REBAR_SESSION_ID=` or `^export AI_AGENT=` (variable-name match, value-independent), on the assumption that only this shim writes those two vars to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE. Document this assumption in the install guide.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documented rationale in the plan (or a manual smoke-test AC) explaining why end-to-end testing against a live Claude Code session is deferred — e.g. 'E2E requires a live API key and is excluded from CI; manual smoke test: install hook per docs, run `claude` in a temp project, and verify `cat $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` contains `REBAR_SESSION_ID`.'
findings.finding: The user-facing flow — hook fires in a real Claude Code session, reads SessionStart stdin, and writes REBAR_SESSION_ID to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE — is covered only by mock/unit tests. No end-to-end check against the actual Claude Code CLI is planned, and no rationale for its absence is documented.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) / Scope bullet: 'a unit test validating the script'
findings.suggested_fix: Append one AC: '**Manual smoke test (not CI-gated):** With the hook installed per `docs/session-id-shims.md`, start a Claude Code session; after SessionStart fires, `cat $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` must contain `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=<non-empty>` and `export AI_AGENT=claude-code`. Proof: a screenshot or terminal capture in the PR description.' Then add a sentence to the Scope bullet: 'End-to-end integration against a live Claude Code session is a manual check (requires API key); not CI-gated.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least a stderr warning (or `--debug`-gated stderr log) for actionable failure modes (python3 not found, env-file write error) so users can diagnose a silent REBAR_SESSION_ID absence without a debugger.
findings.finding: The plan mandates silent no-op (exit 0, no output) for ALL failure modes — parse error, missing python3, unwritable env file — with zero observability. Operators have no way to diagnose why REBAR_SESSION_ID is silently absent.
findings.location: What / Hook script description: 'any parse failure, a missing/blank session_id, or an absent CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is a silent no-op with exit 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Keep exit 0 unconditionally (correct — never block the session), but emit a one-line warning to stderr for non-no-op failures: e.g. `echo '[rebar/session-shim] WARNING: could not write REBAR_SESSION_ID: <reason>' >&2`. This is visible in Claude Code's hook stderr log without blocking anything.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the strip-then-rewrite of CLAUDE_ENV_FILE concurrency-safe — use an atomic write pattern (write to a temp file then `mv`/rename, or wrap the read-modify-write with `flock`) so concurrent subagent SessionStart hook firings cannot interleave and corrupt the file.
findings.finding: The strip-then-rewrite of CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is a non-atomic read-modify-write (read all lines → filter → re-write), with no file locking. The plan itself notes subagents exist (gotcha G1); if two SessionStart hooks fire concurrently for subagents, their interleaved strip/rewrite operations can corrupt or lose each other's writes.
findings.location: What / Hook script description: 'first STRIPS this hook's own prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports from the env file … then re-writes the current values'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `flock <envfile> -c '<read-filter-rewrite>'` (Linux) or write to a per-session temp file then `mv -f` to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE. The `python3` block already in use can do `tempfile + os.replace()` for an atomic rename that is safe on POSIX.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either expand the title/Why section to explicitly acknowledge the vocabulary-alignment scope and note any consumers that matched the old slash-form examples, OR move the vocab-alignment work to a separate plan/ticket to keep this one strictly scoped to the session-shim.
findings.finding: The plan title scopes only to 'populate REBAR_SESSION_ID in the local CLI', but the body also includes a vocabulary-alignment change (updating AI_AGENT examples in docs/config.md, docs/event-schema.md, and session_id.py from the old slash-form to a base-name + optional suffix form). This is undeclared scope in the title and introduces a documentation behavior change whose downstream consumers (anything that pattern-matches the old 'claude-code_<ver>' / 'opencode' slash-form in docs or tooling) are not acknowledged.
findings.location: ## What — Vocabulary alignment bullet; ## Scope — IN list; AC 6
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'Why' section: 'As a companion cleanup, the existing AI_AGENT vocabulary examples in docs and comments are updated from the implicit slash-form (`claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode`) to an explicit base-name + optional `_<version>` suffix form; no runtime behavior changes — only doc/comment text is affected.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (with a citation to Claude Code CLI documentation or a live probe) that `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is the correct env var name exported by the local Claude Code CLI, and add that citation to the plan or install doc.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'with `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is available' as a conditional for writing the versioned harness tag `AI_AGENT=claude-code_<ver>`, but `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` appears zero times anywhere in the codebase. This is an unverified empirical claim about the Claude Code CLI exporting that specific variable name; if the actual env var is different (e.g. `CLAUDE_VERSION` or it is not exported at all), the version suffix will never be written.
findings.location: What section — 'suffixed with `_<version>` (e.g. `claude-code_1.2.3`) when `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is available, else bare `claude-code`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify with ticket 7656 that `docs/session-id-shims.md` is the explicit agreed target, and update 7656's `file_impact` to name that file, or amend this plan's OUT exclusion to state it is not yet confirmed.
findings.finding: The Scope OUT line asserts 'Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)', treating `docs/session-id-shims.md` as the agreed shared target for ticket 7656. However, ticket 7656's `file_impact` only specifies `docs/` (no filename) and its description says only 'Per-harness install docs' — it names no specific file. This is a G-4 scope-exclusion resting on an unverified coordination claim; if 7656 creates a different docs file, the unified install guide never materialises and the exclusion invisibly deletes the requirement.
findings.location: Scope section — OUT: 'Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to both this ticket's description and ticket 7656: 'Shared docs target: `docs/session-id-shims.md`' so the coordination is enforceable and a reviewer can confirm the handoff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.md` (line 218 precedence chain) to the vocabulary-alignment file list so the `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` omission is corrected as part of this story.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.md` line 218 documents the session-id precedence chain as '`REBAR_SESSION_ID`, then `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, then `SESSION_ID`', omitting `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`. The plan's vocabulary-alignment file list ('`docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and the `session_id.py` comment') does not include `CLAUDE.md`, so that file will remain stale after this story ships. The plan asserts the vocabulary-alignment step covers the relevant files, but omits a confirmed-stale file.
findings.location: What section — 'Vocabulary alignment. Update the `claude-code_<ver>` examples in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and the `session_id.py` comment'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section's vocabulary-alignment bullet, change the file list to: '`docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, `CLAUDE.md` (line 218 precedence chain), and the `session_id.py` comment'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether Claude Code exports a version env var, confirm its exact name (cite the Claude Code docs or `claude --version` output), and update the plan's var name accordingly — or explicitly note that `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is a hypothetical and version-suffix support is best-effort.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` (used to derive the `_<version>` suffix on `AI_AGENT`) appears nowhere in the codebase and has no documentation citation. If Claude Code does not export this env var, every installation will emit the bare `claude-code` tag rather than the versioned form, making version-differentiated provenance queries permanently impossible without any diagnostic signal.
findings.location: What section: 'suffixed with `_<version>` (e.g. `claude-code_1.2.3`) when `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` is available'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a repo-wide audit step (e.g., `grep -r 'claude-code_' docs/ README* CHANGELOG*`) to confirm no other files retain the old `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` slash-form after the vocabulary update, and add those files to the update scope if found.
findings.finding: The vocabulary-alignment step names only three files (`docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, `session_id.py`) for the `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` slash-form removal, but does not audit whether the same pattern appears in other docs, READMEs, changelogs, or inline code comments. Any unaudited occurrence would remain stale after the update, creating inconsistent documentation.
findings.location: What section / Scope / AC line 6 ('vocabulary examples in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and `session_id.py`')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope / What section, add: 'Run `grep -rn "claude-code_<ver>\|claude-code_[0-9]" docs/ README* CHANGELOG*` and include any additional hits in the vocabulary-alignment edits.' Optionally add a one-line assertion to `test_harness_provenance.py` that scans the full `docs/` tree for the old slash-form pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief 'Design rationale' section to `docs/session-id-shims.md` (or a lightweight ADR) explaining why the CLAUDE_ENV_FILE append mechanism was chosen over alternatives, so the decision is discoverable.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new hook-based env-injection pattern and a new REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT contract, but does not mention capturing the architectural decision (choice of CLAUDE_ENV_FILE append vs. alternatives) in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc section.
findings.location: Scope / IN list and What / Hook script description
findings.suggested_fix: A short 'Design notes' H2 in docs/session-id-shims.md covering: (1) why CLAUDE_ENV_FILE over process-env injection, (2) the fire-and-forget exit-0 constraint, (3) the strip-then-rewrite idempotency rationale. No separate ADR file needed unless the project has an ADR convention.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the 'What' section explaining *why* `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` + append-with-`>>` is the correct Claude Code env-injection interface (e.g., cite the Claude Code hooks documentation section/version that defines this var, and state why append is correct vs. overwrite so another reviewer can validate the mechanism independently).
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for why the CLAUDE_ENV_FILE append-with->> mechanism is the right interface boundary for env injection — it is stated only as 'the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism' without explaining what property makes it the correct fit (e.g., why not stdout-based hook output, why append vs. truncate, why this var name specifically). This absence means an implementer cannot verify the approach fits or defend it if the Claude Code docs change.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description: 'APPENDS `export REBAR_SESSION_ID=<id>`… (the documented Claude Code env-injection mechanism — append with `>>` so other hooks' vars survive)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the hook-script description, add: 'CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is the env-injection var defined in Claude Code's hooks documentation (https://docs.anthropic.com/…/hooks — §Environment Variables); Claude Code reads this file and exports its contents into the session environment. Append (`>>`) rather than overwrite (`>`) is required because multiple hooks may write to the same file concurrently and overwriting would silently discard the other hooks' exports.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
